utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final coverage plan: It includes health_coverage (with both physical and mental health insurance and costs), property_coverage (home/contents with costs; also a technology_protection sub-item with costs), travel_coverage (travel insurance with costs), additional_services (has services but does not include costs as required), and total_budget (complete monthly/annual cost breakdown provided). Next, verify full specification per item: - health_coverage: includes both physical and mental health insurance, providers, coverage details, and monthly costs → fully specified. - property_coverage: includes home/contents insurance with costs → fully specified. - travel_coverage: includes provider, coverage details, and monthly cost → fully specified. - additional_services: required to include any enhancement services with costs; plan lists services but no costs → not fully specified. - total_budget: includes monthly totals, savings, and annual savings → fully specified. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I identified each item in the final coverage plan and matched its provider name to the closest exact name in the ground-truth ratings. For primary health physical, the plan lists 'LifeFirst Standard Health' which aligns with 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (rating 8). For mental health, the plan lists 'PrimeCare Premium Mental Health' which matches 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' under mental_health (rating 10). For home contents, the plan lists 'PrimeHome Premium' which corresponds to 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' (rating 10). For technology protection, the plan lists 'Technology Protection' which corresponds to the property_enhancement_services item 'Technology Protection' (rating 10). For travel, the plan lists 'PrimeTravel Premium' which maps to 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' (rating 10).
